symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical polyarthritis of wrists, MCP joints and knees

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical, non-erosive small- and large-joint polyarthritis is a defining articular feature of an SLE flare.
Joint sepsis is characteristically monoarticular; a symmetrical polyarthritis argues strongly against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Photosensitive malar rash across both cheeks and the nasal bridge

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A bilateral, photosensitive malar (butterfly) rash sparing the nasolabial folds is a classic cutaneous manifestation of SLE.
Cellulitis is unilateral, spreading and does not respect the nasolabial folds; a symmetrical rash argues against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent ultraviolet (sun) exposure immediately preceding the flare

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: UV light is a well-recognised trigger of cutaneous and systemic SLE flares.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Overwhelming fatigue is one of the most common and disabling features of an SLE flare (systemic cytokine activation), though non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low-grade fever (37.4 C), not high-spiking with rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute flare of systemic lupus erythematosus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of a high-spiking septic fever with rigors makes joint or soft-tissue sepsis less likely, though it does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No single disproportionately hot/red/swollen joint (even symmetrical distribution)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of a dominant hot monoarticular joint is the key discriminator that makes a septic joint unlikely; a positive finding here would mandate urgent escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: No pleuritic chest pain and no shortness of breath

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative pleuritic/breathlessness screen makes active serositis unlikely now; it is the principal deterioration to safety-net for.

symptoms.symptom_name: SLE flare bloods and urinalysis (FBC, ESR/CRP, anti-dsDNA, complement C3/C4, creatinine, urinalysis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rising anti-dsDNA, falling complement, cytopenias and raised inflammatory markers support an active flare and grade its severity; urinalysis screens for nephritis. None are obtainable via telehealth in real time — sealed answer-key investigation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Boggy tender synovitis of wrists/MCPs with bilateral knee effusions; malar rash sparing nasolabial folds; chest clear with no friction rub (in-person examination findings)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: True synovitis with effusions and the classic malar distribution confirm the flare on examination; the clear chest with no rub argues against serositis. These are clinician-measured and sealed — the AI Doctor infers the flare from history and patient-reported swelling, not from a hands-on exam.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute flare of systemic lupus erythematosus
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sun-triggered symmetrical polyarthritis + photosensitive malar rash + fatigue in a known SLE patient is a coherent flare cluster.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Cytopenias and raised inflammatory markers support and grade an SLE flare and help flag intercurrent infection.
Rising anti-dsDNA with falling complement supports serologically active disease and guides immunosuppression.
Screens for lupus nephritis, which is often clinically silent and materially changes management.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Joint aspiration / synovial fluid microscopy and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated in a symmetrical non-septic polyarthritis with no dominant hot joint; reserve for a single disproportionately hot swollen joint (would then be urgent). Over-ordering it here reflects anchoring on sepsis.
